summary: Gruber describes his experience signing up for Apple TV+ using the free year offer that comes with a new Apple device purchase. The process was seamless and took only four taps. However, he uses the occasion to vent about a longstanding Apple Pay confirmation sheet bug that incorrectly wraps his email address, breaking it at a single character 't' — a typographic sin that has bothered him for years. He also notes approvingly that Apple TV+ works via the web at tv.apple.com, which he finds a welcome surprise.
key_points: Apple TV+ signup through the free-year-with-device offer was a seamless four-tap process
A persistent layout bug in Apple Pay's confirmation sheet breaks his email address at a one-character widow, violating basic typographic norms
Apple TV+ being available via web browser at tv.apple.com was an unexpected and welcome feature
The title itself is a quintessential Gruber joke: burying the real complaint in an absurdly long headline
money_quotes: It's as though every single time I confirm an Apple Pay purchase with my phone, Apple throws a little bit of sand into my eyes.
A one-character widow that doesn't even vaguely fall at a natural break — is a violation of every known typographic norm for splitting words that don't fit.
And just look at it — clearly there's enough room for the "t" there. It should fit, which makes it all the more maddening.
It was a simple four-tap process to sign up — fast, easy, and obvious.
It'd be bad enough if the email address were broken between the "daringfireball" and the ".net". A long enough email address has to break somewhere.
key_insight: Even when Apple nails the big experience, persistent small typographic bugs reveal a gap between Apple's design reputation and the details of its own software.
